holeInOne.firstRow: We expected all the letters to be green

twoGuesses.firstRow: We expected the second letter to be absent
twoGuesses.secondRow: We expected all the letters to be green

threeGuesses.firstRow: We expected the second and fourth letters to be absent
threeGuesses.secondRow: We expected the second letter to be absent
threeGuesses.thirdRow: We expected all the letters to be green

phew.firstRow: We expected everything to be absent.
phew.secondRow: We expected everything to be absent.
phew.thirdRow: We expected the last two to be present and the rest absent.
phew.fourthRow: We expected the first to be present and the rest absent.
phew.fifthRow: We expected the second to be present and the rest absent
phew.sixthRow: We expected all the letters to be green

processGame.name: Process a full game
processGame.description: Create a function called processGame that takes a target word and a list of guesses, and calls colorRow for each guess with the correct states.

holeInOne.name: First time!
holeInOne.description: Deal with a first correct guess.

reuseWork.question: How do I reuse my work from `processGuess`?
reuseWork.answer: The logic that turns one guess into a list of states is the same. Pull it into a helper function (or copy it) and call it for each guess in the list.

trackRow.question: How do I track which row each guess belongs to?
trackRow.answer: Loop through the guesses with an index. The row number is the index plus `1` (since `colorRow` expects rows numbered `1-6`).

colourRow.question: How do I actually colour each row?
colourRow.answer: Once you have the `states` list for a guess, call `colorRow(rowNumber, states)`.

fewerGuesses.question: What if the player has made fewer than 6 guesses?
fewerGuesses.answer: Only colour rows for the guesses that exist. The list length tells you how many to process, so you naturally stop at the right point.

colorRow.description: Colors a row on the Wordle board with the given states.

push.description: Adds an element to a list and returns the new list. Does not change the original list.

describers.colorRow: colored a row on the wordle board
